Bourgogne.name: Côte de Beaune Grand Classics
Bourgogne.summary: The historic négociant cellars of Beaune paired with two château estates to the south, covering both legendary Pinot Noir and white Meursault terroirs.

stops.name: Maison Patriarche Père & Fils
stops.visit: Daily self-guided cellar tour with tasting, no booking needed
stops.note: 5 km of candle-lit cellars, the largest in Burgundy, tasting a flight of Côte d'Or appellations

stops.name: Bouchard Père & Fils
stops.note: Cellars beneath the 15th-c. Château de Beaune; taste Beaune Premier Cru from one of Burgundy's largest domaines

stops.name: Maison Joseph Drouhin
stops.visit: Guided tours at set times, booking advised
stops.note: Ancient cellars under old Beaune; tasting of emblematic cuvées including Chablis and Côte d'Or

stops.name: Château de Pommard
stops.visit: Guided tours and tastings, booking required
stops.note: Monopole Clos Marey-Monge; structured Pommard reds with trained guides

stops.name: Château de Meursault
stops.visit: Open daily; tours and salon tastings, reservation advised
stops.note: Grand 14th-c. cellars and a benchmark flight of white Meursault and Côte de Beaune crus

Bourgogne.name: Côte de Nuits Pinot Noir Trail
Bourgogne.summary: A south-to-north drive through the great red-wine communes of the Côte de Nuits, from Nuits-Saint-Georges up to Marsannay at the gates of Dijon.

stops.name: L'Imaginarium
stops.visit: Open daily; interactive museum plus tasting, booking advised
stops.note: Boisset's sparkling and still-wine discovery centre, ideal intro to Burgundy method and terroir

stops.name: Domaine Manuel Olivier
stops.note: Family domaine pouring Côte de Nuits, Côte de Beaune and Hautes-Côtes side by side

stops.name: Château du Clos de Vougeot
stops.note: Cistercian Grand Cru monument and historic wine presses, heart of the Côte de Nuits

stops.name: Château de Gevrey-Chambertin
stops.visit: Guided tours with tasting, by appointment
stops.note: Medieval cellar in the village of the Chambertin Grands Crus; powerful village and 1er Cru reds

stops.name: Château de Marsannay
stops.visit: Open daily; tastings and tours, booking advised
stops.note: Northern gateway estate known for rosé and approachable Marsannay reds

Beaujolais.name: Crus du Beaujolais Loop
Beaujolais.summary: A two-day sweep of the ten Beaujolais crus, from the granite hills of Brouilly in the south up to Fleurie and Moulin-à-Vent in the north.

stops.name: Château de la Chaize
stops.visit: Open for tours and tastings, booking advised
stops.note: Grand 17th-c. estate with a vast vaulted cellar; flagship Brouilly from a single large vineyard

stops.name: Château Thivin
stops.note: Classic family domaine on the slopes of Mont Brouilly; reference Côte de Brouilly

stops.name: Château des Ravatys
stops.note: Historic 19th-c. estate at the foot of Mont Brouilly, Brouilly and Côte de Brouilly

stops.name: Cave de Fleurie
stops.visit: Shop and tasting open most days, no booking needed
stops.note: Cooperative pouring the floral, silky 'queen of Beaujolais' cru Fleurie

stops.name: Le Hameau Duboeuf
stops.visit: Open daily; ticketed wine park with tasting
stops.note: France's largest wine theme park, ending with a tasting flight across the Beaujolais crus

stops.name: Château du Moulin-à-Vent
stops.note: Iconic windmill estate; structured, age-worthy Moulin-à-Vent, the most powerful cru
